_comment: Per-role LLM routing. Two backends: 'ollama' (default — JSON-only fast roles) and 'vllm' (qwen3:30b base + hot-swappable LoRA adapter per request). When backend='vllm' is unavailable, the router silently falls back to Ollama with the base model and NO adapter — see docs/VLLM_SERVING.md ('Fallback behavior'). The legacy flat 'role: model' shape is preserved as a default so existing callers in shark.graph (which read this file directly with _load_model_tiers) keep working unchanged.

routing._comment: Per-role backend + model selection consumed by shark.llm.client.resolve_role_route(). All trading-* roles point at ModelForge-trained adapter tags (hermes3-8b-<role>-current). The router probes Ollama for that tag at resolve time and silently swaps to the 'fallback' base model if the adapter hasn't been published yet — that is how a ModelForge promotion gets auto-picked-up on the next call. Probe cache is 60s, so a fresh publish appears in shark within a minute. Switched from vLLM to Ollama on 2026-05-17 (vLLM crashed the GB10 host on 2026-05-14; Ollama already proved the custom-model pattern works with hermes3:8b-trader). NOTE on naming: published tags use a dash between base+version because Ollama only allows ONE colon per tag (the model:version separator). The action's _slug() does the replacement: `hermes3:8b` base → `hermes3-8b` prefix in the tag.
